title: Background
summary: This page traces the historical development of the number system, from irrational numbers discovered by Pythagoras to negative numbers introduced by medieval Hindu mathematicians and later European bankers, and finally to imaginary/complex numbers formalized by Euler. It explains how each extension of the number system was motivated by the need to solve equations that had no solution in the prior system. The page introduces the engineering notation j = √−1 used in place of i to avoid confusion with electrical current.
